req.title: Hr-Admin deletes department
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path deletion by an authenticated hr-admin (EP – valid partition), (2) unauthenticated deletion attempt returning 401 (NEG), (3) deletion of a department whose name is exactly 1 character – the minimum boundary (BVA), (4) forbidden access when the caller has a non-admin role returning 403 (DT – role/auth decision table), and (5) state transition from 'department exists' through 'deleted' confirming the audit record is written (STC). Tests are self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up departments, use placeholder helpers for auth and audit verification, and assert HTTP status codes plus entity state.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_deletes_existing_department_success
preconditions: A department with a valid name exists in the system
actions: Submit DELETE request to /departments/{id} with hr-admin credentials
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Department no longer exists when queried
Audit record is written for the deletion event
trace: R-WEB-007 – positive outcome: department deleted, audit written, 200/201 returned
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_deletes_existing_department_success(
    hr_admin_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for hr-admin
    create_department,     # <fixture>: creates dept, yields id, cleans up on failure
    audit_log_checker,     # <helper_fn>: queries audit table for event
):
    dept_id = create_department(name="Engineering")

    resp = hr_admin_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(dept_id)

    get_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 404

    assert audit_log_checker(
        action="DELETE",
        entity="department",
        entity_id=dept_id,
    ), "Audit record not found after deletion"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_department
preconditions: A department exists in the system
actions: Create a department via <create_department_fixture> using admin credentials
Submit DELETE request to /departments/{id} without any auth token
expected: Department still exists after the failed attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_department(
    anonymous_client,      # <fixture>: HTTP client with no auth headers
    create_department,     # <fixture>: creates dept, yields id, auto-cleans
    hr_admin_client,       # <fixture>: used only for setup/teardown
    audit_log_checker,     # <helper_fn>
):
    dept_id = create_department(name="Finance")

    resp = anonymous_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert resp.status_code == 401

    get_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Dept must still exist after rejected delete"

    assert not audit_log_checker(
        action="DELETE",
        entity="department",
        entity_id=dept_id,
    ), "No audit record should be written on 401"

tests.name: test_delete_department_with_minimum_length_name
preconditions: A department exists whose name is exactly 1 character (minimum boundary)
actions: Create a department with a 1-character name via <create_department_fixture>
Submit DELETE request to /departments/{id} with hr-admin credentials
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity id
Department no longer exists when queried
trace: R-WEB-007 – boundary: name at minimum length (1 char) must still be deletable
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_delete_department_with_minimum_length_name(
    hr_admin_client,
    create_department,
    audit_log_checker,
):
    min_name = "X"  # 1-character boundary value
    dept_id = create_department(name=min_name)

    resp = hr_admin_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200/201 for min-length name dept, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(dept_id)

    assert hr_admin_client.get(
        f"/departments/{dept_id}"
    ).status_code == 404

    assert audit_log_checker(
        action="DELETE", entity="department", entity_id=dept_id
    )

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_department
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
A department exists in the system
actions: Create a department via <create_department_fixture> using admin credentials
Submit DELETE request to /departments/{id} with employee-role credentials
expected: Department still exists after the failed attempt
trace: R-WEB-007 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_department(
    employee_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client with role=employee
    hr_admin_client,
    create_department,
    audit_log_checker,
):
    dept_id = create_department(name="Legal")

    resp = employee_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")

    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for forbidden role, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    get_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, "Dept must still exist after 403"

    assert not audit_log_checker(
        action="DELETE", entity="department", entity_id=dept_id
    ), "No audit record should be written on 403"

tests.name: test_department_state_transitions_on_delete
actions: Create a department and confirm state is ACTIVE
Check audit log for state change record
expected: Department transitions from ACTIVE to DELETED (or 404 not-found)
Audit record captures state transition with before/after states
trace: R-WEB-007 – state transition: ACTIVE -> DELETED; audit record written
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_department_state_transitions_on_delete(
    hr_admin_client,
    create_department,
    audit_log_checker,
    get_department_state,  # <helper_fn>: returns state string for dept_id
):
    dept_id = create_department(name="Operations")

    initial_state = get_department_state(dept_id)
    assert initial_state == "ACTIVE", f"Unexpected initial state: {initial_state}"

    del_resp = hr_admin_client.delete(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 201)

    post_state_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/departments/{dept_id}")
    assert post_state_resp.status_code in (404, 200)
    if post_state_resp.status_code == 200:
        assert post_state_resp.json().get("state") == "DELETED"

    assert audit_log_checker(
        action="DELETE",
        entity="department",
        entity_id=dept_id,
        extra={"from_state": "ACTIVE"},
    ), "Audit record must capture state transition"
